commands.repair_reason: - [P2] The remaining blocker is maintainer/security-owner acceptance of compatibility-sensitive auth marker semantics and linked-issue scope, not a narrow automated code repair.; Cleared: No concrete supply-chain or secret-exposure regression was found in the source/docs/test diff; the remaining concern is compatibility ownership of the auth marker contract. commands.review_summary:
The PR changes generated env-backed SecretRef provider auth from bare env-var names to `secretref-env:<NAME>` markers, resolves those markers at model-registry request time, updates secrets audit handling for new and stale markers, and refreshes related docs/tests.

PR surface: Source +54, Tests +224, Docs 0. Total +278 across 17 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main resolves auth-profile env SecretRefs to bare env-var strings and the audit flags arbitrary non-marker `models.json` apiKey strings as plaintext. I did not run the reproducer because this review is read-only and tests may write artifacts.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The remaining blocker is maintainer/security-owner acceptance of compatibility-sensitive auth marker semantics and linked-issue scope, not a narrow automated code repair.
